title: verbascum × bastardii subsp. pilosum
intro: Verbascum × bastardii subsp. pilosum, also known as mullein, is a biennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. Mullein's health benefits include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality. Mullein can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical creams and ointments for skin issues.
